People: 2145: Edwd Kelly (lds) returned on board and reported having been cut by Robt. Shaw (lds).  At 10pm rec'd information that John Gibney (OSer) has been killed while on shore. Sent mate W.N. Smith with Sergant Worth and one private to ascertain about it. At 11 they returned and reported having found the man dead and the body awaiting inquest. Sent Asst Surgeon D Dickinson to ascertain the cause of death. He reported on his return that John Gibney had died from a wound inflicted by Robt. Shaw.  1130PM received information that Robt. Shaw had been arrested for committing the deed, also that 5 other men were wounded. Sent mate W.N. Smith and Surgeon D Dickinson with 5 men to remove the body and assist the wounded if found.
1215: Sergt returned on board with Richard Kugan Coppersmith severly wounder and George Olden (sea Ex)  1245 Mr Smith, Dr Dickinson and Sergt returned on board with ? Webb (Oser)
1600: At 4 Sent Funeral provission in charge of Master S.M. Ackley. Ord Sergt one Corpl and 6 Private Marines and 46 men to bury John Gibney (OSer)

1800: Got Underway. This trip makes no sense. I ignore his observed Lat/Long. It has to be further west to pass to the west of the island. Also, he can't go 5 n.m. in 10 minutes. I think he anchored at 1710 not 1610. He also needs another five miles to get to Port of Spain.

Other: 1330: Fired twelve Broadsides with the Great guns. Broke 3 deck planks under No4 gun port side & disabled the carriage for use. On examination was found to have been struck in thirty one places by fragments.
